Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • Beginner • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Six persons A, B, C, D, E and F are standing in a row. C and D are standing close to each other alongside E. B is standing beside A only. A is fourth from F. Who are standing on the extremes?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
As per data given,
CDE will be together in some order i.e.
CDE or ECD or DCE or EDC
Since, A is fourth from F, the possible combinations are
F _ _ _ A_ or _F _ _ _ A
or
Since E, C and D are together and B is besides A, the possible combination is F(ECD)AB.
Thus, B and F have to be at extremes.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) can be used only for unsupervised learning tasks.
S2 : Decision Trees provide a clear and interpretable structure for decision-making processes.
Which of the above is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) are mainly used for supervised learning tasks not unsupervised ones. They can be adapted for unsupervised tasks in some cases but this is not their most common use. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Decision Tree provide a clear and interpretable structure for decision-making processes, making it easy to understand how decision are made. So, S2 is correct.

Q30 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1 : Roll-up operation in OLAP summarizes data by moving up a hierarchy, leading to higherlevel aggregates.
S2 : Drill-down in OLAP increases data detail by navigating down a hierarchy, revealing finer granularity.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The roll-up operation in OLAP (Online Analytical Processing) aggregates data by moving up the hierarchy levels (e.g., from individual sales to regional totals). So, S1 is correct.
• The drill-down operation in OLAP allows users to view more detailed data by moving down the hierarchy (e.g., from regional totals to individual sales). So, S2 is correct.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• Ridge Regression uses L2 regularization, which adds a penalty proportional to the sum of the square of coefficients, not their absolute value. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• PCA reduces dimensionality by transforming the original features into orthogonal component that capture the most variance in the data. So, option (b) is correct.
• Random Forests is an ensemble method that combines predictions from multiple decision tree. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Gradient Descent is used to optimize the parameters of a neural network by iteratively adjusting weight to minimize the loss function. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q44 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1 : In IDA*, the search is repeated with increasing depth limits until the solution is found.
S2 : A* search is uses a Priority queue for node expansion.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Iterative Deepening A* (IDA*) performs a series of depth-first searches with increasing depth limits based on the f-cost until it finds the solution. So, S1 is correct.
• A* search algorithm uses a priority queue (often implemented as a min-heap) to keep track of nodes to be expanded based on their f-cost values. So, S2 is correct.

Q49 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?
S1 : In Ridge Regression, regularization is applied to prevent overfitting by penalizing large coefficients.
S1 : The k-NN algorithm performs well with high-dimensional data due to the curse of dimensionality.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Ridge Regression includes a regularization the L2 norm of the coefficients to be penalize large coefficients, which helps to prevent overfitting. So, S1 is true.
• The k-NN algorithm does not performs well with high-dimensional data because the curse of dimensionality makes distance between points less meaningful leading to poor performance. So, S2 is false.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
• Naive Bayes assumes features are independent given the class label. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Hierarchical clustering include methods like single-linkage (minimum distance) and completelinkage (maximum distance) approaches. So, option (b) is correct.
• Naive Bayes is for classifier, not dimensionality reduction, PCA is responsible for dimensionality reduction. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Hierarchical clustering does not require specifying the number of cluster before hand. So, option (d) is incorrect.
